description:
A localization engineer has delivered an unsorted French-language JSON file that lives at
    /home/user/project/i18n/fr.json

    Your goal is to prepare this file for import into the company’s translation
    management system (TMS).  The TMS has two strict requirements:

    1) All keys in the JSON must be sorted in simple **lexicographical
       (byte-wise) ascending order**.
    2) A companion CSV file must exist alongside the JSON.  
       • The CSV must be named fr_sorted.csv  
       • It must contain a single header line:  

         key,fr  

       • Each subsequent line must contain the key, a literal comma, and the
         French string value taken from the JSON.  
       • **Keys and values may never be quoted**, even if they contain accented
         characters.  
       • Lines are terminated by a single UNIX newline (`\n`), and no final
         blank line is allowed.

    What you have to deliver
    ------------------------
    After finishing your work, the directory /home/user/project/i18n/ **must
    contain three files**:

    1. fr.json            (unchanged original file)  
    2. fr_sorted.json     (your new file; keys sorted; two-space indentation; a
       trailing newline)  
    3. fr_sorted.csv      (your new CSV file matching the exact format described
       above)

    Quality check log
    -----------------
    To help the build server verify your work, create a log file  
    /home/user/project/i18n/process.log with exactly **one single line**:

        Processed <N> keys from fr.json into fr_sorted.json and fr_sorted.csv

    Replace <N> with the number of translation pairs that exist in the
    original fr.json (do not count comments or whitespace).  The log line must
    end with a newline and **no other text is allowed**.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • You do NOT have root privileges.  
    • You should need only two or three shell commands to complete the task.  
    • Do not modify fr.json.  
    • File and directory permissions must stay at their default user-level
      values.

    Example of expected CSV layout (values are illustrative)

        key,fr
        AddButton,Ajouter
        CancelButton,Annuler
        ...etc.

    When everything is in place, notify me that the task is complete.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts
    --------------------------------------------------------
    Path: /home/user/project/i18n/fr.json  
    Contents (with one trailing newline):

    {
      "CancelButton": "Annuler",
      "AddButton": "Ajouter",
      "DeleteDialogTitle": "Supprimer l’élément ?",
      "EditLabel": "Modifier",
      "SaveSuccess": "Enregistré avec succès"
    }

    Ground-truth expectations after agent finishes
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/project/i18n/fr_sorted.json  
       EXACT contents (including final newline):

    {
      "AddButton": "Ajouter",
      "CancelButton": "Annuler",
      "DeleteDialogTitle": "Supprimer l’élément ?",
      "EditLabel": "Modifier",
      "SaveSuccess": "Enregistré avec succès"
    }

    2. /home/user/project/i18n/fr_sorted.csv  
       EXACT contents (including final newline, no extra blank lines):

    key,fr
    AddButton,Ajouter
    CancelButton,Annuler
    DeleteDialogTitle,Supprimer l’élément ?
    EditLabel,Modifier
    SaveSuccess,Enregistré avec succès

    3. /home/user/project/i18n/process.log  
       EXACT contents (including final newline):

    Processed 5 keys from fr.json into fr_sorted.json and fr_sorted.csv

    Notes for the automated checker
    -------------------------------
    • It should confirm that fr.json remains exactly as it was.  
    • It should compare fr_sorted.json and fr_sorted.csv byte-for-byte with the
      strings above.  
    • process.log must match exactly the single-line template, with “5” in place
      of <N>.
